name: Stir-Fried Shrimp with Garlic and Green Beans
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp fresh ginger, peeled and minced
1/4 cup scallions, minced (use both green and white parts)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the shrimp and simmer the shells for about 5 minutes in water while you prepare the other ingredients.
2. Marinate the shrimp with a mixture of 1/2 teaspoon sugar, 1 tablespoon soy sauce, sliced garlic, salt (if using), sherry, and 1 teaspoon sesame oil.
3. Drain the shrimp shells, reserving 3/4 cup of the stock for later use.
4. If the green beans are tough, parboil them briefly, then plunge into ice water. Cut large beans into pieces.
5. Preheat a wok or 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat for 3 minutes. Add 1 tablespoon peanut oil and raise the heat to medium high until it begins to smoke.
6. Add minced garlic, followed immediately by the shrimp and its marinade. Cook the shrimp about 1 minute per side.
7. Remove the cooked shrimp with a spatula and set aside.
8. In the same wok, add remaining peanut oil over high heat, then ginger. Stir-fry for about 30 seconds.
9. Add the green beans and cook, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned, about 3 to 5 minutes.
10. Return the shrimp and its cooking liquid (about 1/4 cup) to the wok. Let it bubble gently for 1 or 2 minutes.
11. Stir in chili-garlic paste, scallions, remaining sugar, and soy sauce. Cook for an additional minute.
12. Turn off the heat, drizzle with lime juice, and give a final stir. Serve hot.
